data.title: Moore Threads' shares surge over 500% in Shanghai IPO, raising 8 billion yuan ($1.13 billion), dubbed 'China's Nvidia.'
data.description: Chinese chipmaker Moore Threads Technology, likened to China's Nvidia, saw its shares surge by 502% upon its debut on the Shanghai stock exchange. The shares opened at 650 yuan ($91.92), a significant rise from the IPO price of 114.28 yuan ($16.16), with the stock later touching 688 yuan during the session. The IPO was oversubscribed by over 4,000 times, raising 8 billion yuan ($1.13 billion) in the mainland's second-largest IPO of the year. Despite being currently unprofitable, the company witnessed revenue growth of over 300% in 2024. The IPO proceeds will focus on accelerating core R&D, especially in self-developed AI GPUs. This trend aligns with China's aim to reduce reliance on U.S. chipmakers like Nvidia. Moore Threads obtained fast CSRC approval and received substantial backing, including a significant pre-IPO investment from DeepSeek and HighFlyer founder Liang Wenfeng. Founded by a former Nvidia executive, Zhang Jianzhong, the company faced U.S. sanctions in 2023 limiting its access to advanced chip manufacturing processes. Other Chinese players in the AI chip sector include Huawei and Cambricon.

data.title: Nasdaq rises 50 points. Fear index remains high. U.S. stocks mixed. Fed expected to cut rates by 25 basis points with 90% probability.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed indicator revealed a slight decrease in overall fear but remained in the "Fear" zone on Thursday. In the U.S. stock market, the Nasdaq Composite rose by approximately 50 points before the widely anticipated Federal Reserve rate cut next week. Traders are highly confident in a third consecutive 25-basis-point reduction by the Fed, with futures indicating a likelihood of nearly 90%. Dollar General Corp. (NYSE: DG) was the top gainer in the S&P 500, surging by 14% after surpassing earnings expectations and offering optimistic guidance, reaching its highest point since August 2024. Additionally, Salesforce Inc. (NASDAQ: CRM) saw a 4% increase in value following favorable results. For further details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Heineken appoints Alex Carreteiro as Regional President Americas, replacing Marc Busain, effective 1 March 2026. Carreteiro previously served as CEO of PepsiCo Brazil & South Cone Foods Business, overseeing 16,000 employees and 10 factories across multiple countries, achieving significant market share gains and recognition as PepsiCo's Global Business Unit of the Year in 2024. Carreteiro also held senior roles at Nestlé. The appointment aims to drive growth in the Americas region, critical to Heineken's long-term EverGreen strategy, with a focus on operational excellence and people-first leadership.CEO Dolf van den Brink expressed enthusiasm for Carreteiro's joining the team.
data.description: Heineken N.V. has appointed Alex Carreteiro as the new Regional President Americas, taking over from Marc Busain on 1 March 2026. Alex, currently the CEO of PepsiCo Brazil & South Cone Foods Business, has a track record of success including doubling the size of PepsiCo’s Brazil business and achieving significant market share gains. He has experience at Nestlé as well, with senior roles in General Management, Sales, and Finance in Europe and the Americas. Alex's appointment aims to accelerate growth in the Americas region critical to Heineken’s long-term strategy. With 16,000 employees and 10 factories under his responsibility, Alex's expertise in beverages and food, and experience in M&A and team-building make him a valuable addition to the Heineken team. CEO Dolf van den Brink expressed excitement about Alex joining the team, praising his strategic vision and operational skills as assets that align well with Heineken’s values and future goals.
